schemas.name: Virtual Auditor
schemas.description: AI-powered CA and IBBI Registered Valuer firm offering IBBI-compliant valuations (ESOP, FEMA, Income Tax, Companies Act, SEBI, IBC, 409A, complex instruments, AIF), FEMA compliance, GST and income tax appeals, forensic accounting, company registration, and secretarial services across India.

address.streetAddress: G-131, Ground Floor, Phase 3, Spencer Plaza Mall, Anna Salai

schemas.name: Income Tax Return Filing — ITR 1-7
schemas.description: Income tax return filing by CA firm. ITR-1 to ITR-7 for individuals, HUFs, companies, LLPs, trusts. Tax planning advisory. Due date compliance. Virtual Auditor.

schemas.name: Income Tax Return Filing
schemas.description: Income tax return filing by CA firm. ITR-1 to ITR-7 for individuals, HUFs, companies, LLPs, trusts. Tax planning advisory. Due date compliance. Virtual Auditor.

step.text: Reconcile income, TDS, and advance tax
step.name: Step 2

step.text: File online on e-filing portal with DSC/EVC
step.name: Step 4

mainEntity.name: What is the due date for ITR filing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Non-audit cases (ITR-1 to ITR-4 for individuals): July 31. Audit cases (companies, firms requiring audit): October 31. Transfer pricing cases: November 30. Belated returns: December 31 (with late fee).

mainEntity.name: Can you help with old/belated returns?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Belated returns can be filed up to December 31 of the assessment year (with \u20b95,000 late fee under Section 234F). Revised returns can be filed up to December 31 to correct errors in the original return.

mainEntity.name: Do you provide tax planning advisory?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. We review your income, deductions, investments, and capital gains before filing and advise on: Section 80C/80D optimisation, capital gains tax harvesting, new vs. old regime comparison, and advance tax planning.

mainEntity.name: Which ITR form should I file?

acceptedAnswer.text: ITR-1 (Sahaj): salary + 1 house property + other sources, income up to ₹50 lakhs. ITR-2: capital gains, multiple house properties, foreign income. ITR-3: business/profession income. ITR-4 (Sugam): presumptive taxation. ITR-5: LLPs/firms. ITR-6: companies. ITR-7: trusts/institutions.

mainEntity.name: What is the due date for income tax return filing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Non-audit: July 31. Audit cases (Section 44AB): October 31. Transfer pricing cases: November 30. Belated return: December 31 (with ₹5,000 late fee under Section 234F; ₹1,000 if income under ₹5 lakhs).

mainEntity.name: What is the penalty for late ITR filing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Late fee under Section 234F: ₹5,000 (if filed by December 31) or ₹10,000 (after December 31). Reduced to ₹1,000 if total income does not exceed ₹5 lakhs. Interest under Section 234A: 1% per month on unpaid tax.

mainEntity.name: Can I revise my income tax return?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Under Section 139(5), you can file a revised return before the end of the assessment year (March 31). Updated return under Section 139(8A) can be filed within 24 months of end of assessment year (with additional tax of 25-50%).

mainEntity.name: Is tax audit mandatory for my business?

acceptedAnswer.text: Mandatory under Section 44AB if: (1) business turnover exceeds ₹1 crore (₹10 crore if 95% digital transactions), or (2) professional receipts exceed ₹50 lakhs, or (3) opted out of presumptive taxation with income below presumptive limit.
